matcher.coveragePreview: surface areas and volumes: 3-d shapes middle school mathematics students surface areas: cube, cuboid, cylinder volumes: cone, sphere, hemisphere composite shapes surface areas and volumes: 3-d shapes introduction to session topic session: surface areas & volumes (3-d shapes) key focus: cube, cuboid, cylinder, cone, sphere what we will learn today learning objectives identify properties of 3d shapes (faces, edges, vertices). recall formulas for tsa and volume of cube, cuboid, cylinder. calculate surface area and volume of cone, sphere, hemisphere. basic solids: cube & cuboid cube and cuboid formulas [object object] cuboid: length(l), width(w), height(h). tsa = 2(lw + wh + lh) volume = l × w × h the cylinder: curved surfaces cylinder surface area and volume radius = r, height = h. curved surface area (csa): 2πrh total surface area (tsa): 2πr(r + h) volume: πr²h cones and spheres in action cone and sphere calculations [object object] [object object] example: find volume of a sphere with radius 7cm. composite solids challenge composite shapes application problem: a tent is a cylinder with a conical top. step 1: calculate cylinder csa (2πrh). step 2: calculate cone csa (πrl). total a...
